description_long: This utility pole is a tall, cylindrical structure made primarily of dark, weathered wood, featuring a horizontal crossarm near the top with four insulator mounts. Below the crossarm, a large, boxy electrical component is attached, with several wires and cables extending from it. The pole has a rugged, utilitarian appearance, with visible bolts and metal brackets securing the crossarm and electrical box. The wires are bundled and looped, suggesting connections for power distribution or communication. The surface of the pole is textured, showing signs of exposure to the elements, and the metal parts have a slightly rusted, industrial look. The overall design is typical of utility poles found along urban streets, supporting electrical infrastructure.
description: A weathered wooden utility pole with a horizontal crossarm, insulator mounts, and a large electrical box with attached wires. The pole features rugged metal fittings and a utilitarian, industrial appearance.
description_view_0: Front view showing the crossarm with four insulator mounts and the large electrical box with attached wires clearly visible.
description_view_1: Side view revealing the slim profile of the pole and the protruding electrical box, with wires extending outward.
description_view_2: Rear view displaying the back of the crossarm and the pole, with the electrical box and wires partially visible.
description_view_3: Opposite side view highlighting the electrical box's depth and the arrangement of wires, with minimal crossarm detail.
attribute_arrangement_of_crossarms: A single horizontal crossarm is mounted near the top of the pole, perpendicular to the main shaft.
